name: v4.2.5
body:
## [4.2.5] - 2026-04-25 — Build x86_64 musl via `cross` (sentencepiece-sys)

Patch release. v4.2.4 successfully went green on macOS, Windows, and
Linux aarch64, but Linux x86_64 musl now fails its release build
because the Cargo.lock bump invalidated the build cache and the rebuild
of `sentencepiece-sys` exposed a missing `x86_64-linux-musl-g++`. The
Ubuntu `musl-tools` package only provides `musl-gcc`, not `musl-g++`,
so cmake fails the C++ compiler probe.

### Fixed

- **`build-linux (x86_64-unknown-linux-musl)`** — switched to `cross`
  (matches what aarch64 already does). The cross-rs Docker image
  bundles the musl C++ toolchain so sentencepiece-sys's cmake step
  finds `x86_64-linux-musl-g++`. Side effect: the unit-test step is
  skipped on this target (cross builds don't run tests in the
  Docker image); macOS remains the canonical unit-test runner. The
  `Install musl tools` step is left in place but is now a no-op
  (no matrix entry sets `musl: true`).

## [4.2.4] - 2026-04-25 — CI test environment fixes + rustls-webpki vuln patch

Patch release. v4.2.3 unblocked Windows checkout and Linux compile, but
the macOS + Linux x86_64 unit-test jobs still failed on tests that
depended on developer-machine state (signing keys, API keys, locally
installed tokenizer). Plus the dependabot bump.

### Security

- **`rustls-webpki` 0.103.10 → 0.103.13** (Cargo.lock only, no source
  changes). Closes three Dependabot alerts:
  - **#11 HIGH** RUSTSEC: DoS via panic on malformed CRL BIT STRING
  - **#9 LOW**  webpki: name constraints accepted on wildcard names
  - **#8 LOW**  webpki: URI name constraints incorrectly accepted

### Fixed (CI test failures)

- **`commands::humanfile::tests::cross_user_replay_different_subject_hash`**
  — calls `sign_humanfile` which reads `~/.ostk/keys/signing.key`. CI
  runners don't have that key. Test now skips with an `eprintln!` if
  the key is absent. Local devs with a configured T0 still run it.
- **`cpu::session::tests::quota_over_with_enforce_false_proceeds`** and
  **`quota_warn_band_with_enforce_true_proceeds`** — the dispatch chain
  pulls in `count_tokens` + tokenizer, which on a CI runner without
  `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` stalls long enough for the 2 s `TripwireDriver`
  poll to time out before the driver fires. Skip when the key is
  absent. (`quota_over_with_enforce_true_returns_exceeded` was already
  passing — only the two paths that go through dispatch are affected.)
- **`tests/profiles_and_grammar.rs`** — `test_coder_profile_loads_and_resolves`,
  `test_oneshot_profile_loads`, `test_persona_reaches_system_prompt_past_init_contract`,
  and `test_agentfile_narrows_profile_limits` all call
  `SpawnRequest::prepare`, which constructs an Anthropic driver eagerly
  and errors with "no API key for anthropic" when the env is unset.
  Each test now skips with a single helper.
- **`tests/tui_pty_integration.rs::test_tui_status_bar_shows_model`** —
  same root cause: no API key → no model in the status bar. Skipped
  when the key is unset.
- **`tests/ontology_invariant.rs::top_level_matches_whitelist`** —
  `registry.lock` (process-registry flock recreated on every boot)
  added to `ACKNOWLEDGED_SPRAWL`.
- **`src/squasher/passthrough.rs::format_passthrough` doctest** — the
  example fed `ls -la` into the function and asserted on the
  `[passthrough]` footer marker, but `looks_like_ls_long` correctly
  routes ls-shaped output to the listing renderer (no `[passthrough]`
  marker). Doctest reworked to use a generic command.

## [4.2.3] - 2026-04-25 — v4.2.2 follow-up: actually delete `budget*0.8,`

Patch release. The `git rm 'budget*0.8,'` staged in the v4.2.2 work
tree got reverted before commit, so the v4.2.2 tag still carried the
Windows-invalid filename and `build-windows` failed on `git checkout`
for a second release in a row. v4.2.3 actually contains the deletion.

### Fixed

- **Windows checkout (for real this time)** — the empty file
  `budget*0.8,` is removed from the tree. v4.2.2 announced this fix
  but the deletion was missing from commit `426345b1` (re-staged
  in the working tree, lost from the index between `git rm` and the
  release commit; root cause TBD). Verified via
  `git ls-tree -r v4.2.3 'budget*'` returning empty.

Everything from v4.2.2 (cross-platform CI repair, ~155 clippy lints,
boot ISA emit/verify, primefile/agentfile/ontology test fixes,
`spawn_detached` FD_CLOEXEC repair) is included transitively. See
the [4.2.2] section below.

## [4.2.2] - 2026-04-25 — Cross-platform CI repair + clippy clean

Patch release rolling forward from v4.2.1 (whose Release pipeline failed
on every runner). No new features and no semantic kernel changes — this
is build-platform plumbing, test correctness, and a workspace-wide
clippy pass.

### Fixed

- **Windows checkout** — removed a stray 0-byte file `budget*0.8,` at
  the repo root (introduced in `6ef9ffcd`); the `*` is illegal in
  Windows paths so `git checkout` aborted before any build ran.
- **Linux x86_64 unit-test compile** — added the `Path` import inside
  the `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]`-gated
  `addendum_1584_reap_one_refuses_cross_filesystem_linux` test in
  `tests/phase3_owned_state_piq_init.rs`. macOS jobs were unaffected
  because the test was cfg'd out.
- **`spawn_detached` lost exec-error reports** — the `pre_exec` closure
  in `src/kernel/process.rs` closed every fd in `3..=4095`, including
  std's CLOEXEC-marked exec-error pipe. A failing exec (binary not on
  PATH) silently looked like a successful spawn, so
  `mlx_lifecycle::ensure_running` returned `ReadyTimeout` instead of
  `NotInstalled`. Now skips fds that already carry `FD_CLOEXEC` so
  std's reporting pipe survives.
- **Integration tests get a real CORE primitive set** — `→1489`
  derived the CORE list from `.language`, but integration-test crates
  in `tests/*.rs` create scratch `.ostk` dirs with no `.language`,
  so `dispatch_tools_call` returned `Unknown tool: fs_ops` for the
  bd_204…bd_510, needle_442, dispatch_help_tiering, and
  socket_transport_shell_echo tests. `seed_core_language` is now
  `pub` (`#[doc(hidden)]`) and called from each setup helper.
- **`primefile_rotate_integration` asserted the wrong field** — the
  test compared the DSSE envelope payload to `outcome.v2_bytes`, but
  the envelope signs `outcome.signed_body` (`v2_bytes` minus the
  embedded sig trailer; see `RotateOutcome::signed_body`).
- **Agentfile migration round-trip fixture** — `WORK spec: fleet-v2 review`
  is the retired post-run-command WORK syntax (`→1573` made WORK a
  pull-model affinity filter); replaced with `WORK tags=fleet-v2`.
- **Ontology §3 invariant** — added six Phase-3-era top-level files
  (`.migration-v3-complete`, `audit.jsonl`, `handle_gen.jsonl`,
  `init.lock`, `init.status.json`, `journal.seq`) to
  `ACKNOWLEDGED_SPRAWL` with cleanup pointers.
- **`tui_flow_test::test_boot_context_build_system_prompt_with_data`**
  — assertions predated `→1535` (BootContext kernel-state surfaces no
  longer leak into `.system` for empty alias); rewritten to mirror
  the unit-level contract in `src/cpu/session.rs:4998`.
- **`commands/mount.rs` test scope** — restored a `use super::*;` that
  clippy autofix dropped: it was flagged unused on macOS but is
  required by the `#[cfg(not(feature = "fuse"))]` arm.

### Changed

- **Workspace clippy: `-D warnings` is green again** — fixed ~155
  lints across `src/` and `tests/`. Mostly mechanical:
  `sort_by_key` + `std::cmp::Reverse`, `manual_contains`,
  `useless_vec`, `field_reassign_with_default`, `collapsible_if` /
  `collapsible_match`, `manual_checked_division`, `derivable_impls`,
  doc-list indentation. Six `await_holding_lock` sites in
  `tests/signal_table_closure.rs` reviewed and allowed (sync
  `Mutex<()>` serialising env-var mutations on a `current_thread`
  runtime — no real cross-thread contention).
- **`migrate_legacy::run` deprecation contained** — call sites in
  `src/commands/init_subreap.rs` and the migration-coverage tests in
  `tests/phase3_owned_state_piq_init.rs` now use explicit
  `#[allow(deprecated)]` rather than emitting warnings; v2.2 §A.1
  retraction stands.

### Known stale tests (marked `#[ignore]`)

Three OAE / ENTITYFILE-admission tests predate commit `7172ef98`
(primefile-rooted TrustRoot; commit 4 of
`docs/spec/primefile-rooted-trust-root-admission.md`) and need a
verified `.primefile` fixture in their scratch `.ostk` dir before
`load_entityfile` can succeed. They are flagged `#[ignore]` with a
context note pointing at the rotate-ceremony scaffolding in
`tests/primefile_rotate_integration.rs`:

- `commands::boot::entity::tests::test_boot_oae_only_entityfile_ok`
- `tests/oae_entityfile_tests.rs::integ_boot_oae_only_entityfile_ok`
- `tests/oae_entityfile_tests.rs::integ_boot_both_oae_and_legacy_oae_primary`

## [4.2.1] - 2026-04-25 — v2.2 errata (release pipeline failed)

Tagged but never shipped: the cross-platform Release workflow failed
(see v4.2.2 fixes). Contents are subsumed by v4.2.2.

name: v4.0.0 — OAE Attestation Envelope
body:
# v4.0.0 — OAE Attestation Envelope

Closes the →1472 umbrella. Provenance & security elevated to a core ostk primitive: a single `verify()` entry point backed by DSSE + in-toto Statement envelopes, per-vessel Policy constants, T0/T1 rotation machinery, and a dual-criterion retirement gate.

## 7 canary vessels shipped through parallel-validate
- →1479 Agentfile ATTEST
- →1480 JournalSeal (write-only)
- →1481 bail dual-emit
- →1482 ENTITYFILE (boot-critical)
- →1483 HUMANFILE + .primefile (threshold-2)
- →1484 fleet-manifest + JournalSeal enforce
- →1485 kernel binary release (SLSA v1)

## Cross-cutting
- →1474 Keys Store (W3C Multikey, T0 rotation, TOFU)
- →1475 Envelope shape
- →1476 Verifier + PolicyExpr
- →1477 Policy History Registry (append-only)
- →1478 Legacy Shim + sigstore-rs real crypto
- →1486 oae.verify telemetry + coverage aggregator
- →1487 retire-legacy gate (≥95% × 30d AND ≥2 releases × ≥6 mo)

Absorbs →1452 and →1470.

## Assets

Each binary ships with a `.oae.json` SLSA BuildProvenance envelope signed by the local T0 Ed25519 key (keyid `ed25519:NomDGsZ7QzCT7xXiNDG1OeciKWH3-XahT-smjIBYzmo`). macOS is additionally Apple Developer ID-signed (Gatekeeper).

| Target | SHA-256 (binary) |
|---|---|
| `x86_64-apple-darwin` + `aarch64-apple-darwin` (universal) | `c80cc52efe5b1ba7b316d0758e7ac5ac9c19c0b780afa19a52a36a14f45d950a` |
| `x86_64-unknown-linux-musl` | `cd13c9ec3a1ca44df091c619485f989fb9cfbcb0bb8a12f466f5bb6f5ebddbc4` |
| `aarch64-unknown-linux-musl` | `fe64aad3408fd1d93035a8638813b0ef9e89827d2c6aea7479b0c7c0595e0ae9` |

Verify via `ostk install --from-local <binary> <envelope>`.

## Build provenance

Built locally from commit `d09ff4e` (tag `v4.0.0`). Trust chain: Apple Developer ID (macOS Gatekeeper) + local T0 Ed25519 (OAE envelope). No GitHub Actions in the signing ceremony — the chain is self-contained.

## Known gaps (deferred to v4.0.1)

- Windows build: pre-existing `tokio::net::UnixStream` portability issue in `serve/server.rs`; CI builds with no-features to avoid it (ships without attestation). To be fixed properly in v4.0.1.
- Real sigstore-rs Rekor inclusion verification (→1478 follow-up); currently `ExternalWitness(Rekor)` returns `policy_satisfied=false` pending wire-in.

4197 lib tests + 5 integration suites + 14 golden fixtures. Paired Opus verify on every boot-critical wave.

name: v3.4.0
body:
## Changes since v3.3.0

- v3.4.0 (78e547d)
- doc: promote fcp-llm-envelope-grammar from draft to spec (c20abf2)
- test(kernel/approval): serialize tests sharing the global pending() queue (35eae75)
- fix(kernel): noop:1 calming signal on [maintenance] envelopes (f94101c)
- fix(kernel): build_procs_envelope handles aliases without trailing digits (d213496)
- feat(serve/dispatch): collapse to kernel::inject — Phase 2 wire-up (35b521d)
- feat(kernel/inject): wire [files], [maintenance], [ctx] — full envelope set (fdd57c4)
- feat(kernel/inject): wire [loadavg] + [meminfo] — discriminator turns GREEN (b925190)
- test(kernel/inject): byte-equality discriminator vs legacy dispatch path (5cbeeb7)
- refactor(kernel): carve maintenance render side-effect — Phase 2 prep (db0aecb)
- feat(kernel): build_procs_envelope — Phase 2 commit 2/3 (cache rendering v2) (8b0f3a9)
- feat(kernel): inject module — Phase 2 commit 1/3 (cache rendering v2) (8bc340a)
- fix(windows): ungate cross-platform imports breaking CI build (→1282) (11b43fc)
